_about: Curated, AUTHORITATIVE seed for the Vedic KG. Sources are indigenous Vedic apparatus (the anukramaṇī hymn-indices, the Bṛhaddevatā/itihāsa tradition) and standard entity identifications — NOT colonial translations, so this stays consistent with the project's anti-translator-bias mission. Loaded with provenance='authoritative' so the self-building (inferred) layer can never overwrite it. Keep every entry verifiable; do not add speculative genealogy.

facts.subject: Asamati
facts.note: asamātiṣu rathaproṣṭheṣu — name + patronymic in the clan-plural

facts.subject: Asamati
facts.note: the praised patron of the hymn; addressed rājan in 10.60.6

facts.subject: Subandhu
facts.object: Gaupayana rishi revived in the Subandhu legend

facts.subject: Trkshi
facts.note: Trāsadasyava is the vṛddhi patronymic of Trasadasyu — 'of Trasadasyu's line'. Grammatically certain; the person-vs-clan reading is not.

facts.subject: Trkshi
facts.object: Rigvedic name (RV 8.22.7; also RV 6.46.8), appearing as 'tṛkṣiṃ trāsadasyavam' — of the Trasadasyu line, sped by the Aśvins to great kṣatra (dominion). DISPUTED: read either as an individual prince descended from Trasadasyu (Vedic Index) or as a tribe/clan of his descendants (Talageri).

facts.subject: Harishchandra
facts.object: Ikshvaku king of the Shunahshepa legend

10.060.rishi: the Gaupāyanas — Bandhu, Subandhu, Śrutabandhu and Viprabandhu
10.060.devata: Subandhu's revival; the king Asamāti; the hand (v.12)
10.060.theme: Praise of the king Asamāti and the bringing-back-to-life of the slain Gaupāyana Subandhu (the Subandhu/Gaupāyana legend). v.3 describes a warlike sovereign; v.4 names Ikṣvāku; v.6 addresses 'rājan' (O king); vv.7-10 are the revival.
10.060.legend: The Gaupāyana brothers serve King Asamāti; when Subandhu is struck dead, his brothers revive him by their hymns (vv. 7-12).

7.018.theme: The Battle of the Ten Kings (dāśarājña): Sudās's victory on the Paruṣṇī.

3.062.devata: various; v.10 (the Gāyatrī) is to Savitṛ

AVŚ 20.127.theme: One of the Kuntāpa hymns: praise of King Parikṣit and his flourishing Kuru realm ('the people of the Kuru king'). A rare named historical king in the Atharvaveda.

AVŚ 5.22.theme: A charm to banish takman (fever) to distant peoples — it names the Gandhāris, Mūjavants, Aṅgas, Magadhas (and Balhikas): a NW↔east spread of peoples and a key geographic/ethnographic passage.

1.024.theme: Hymns of Śunaḥśepa to Varuṇa and other gods (the Śunaḥśepa cluster, RV 1.24-30).
1.024.legend: Śunaḥśepa, son of Ajīgarta, was bought by the Ikṣvāku king Hariścandra to be offered in place of his son Rohita; bound to the stake, he praised the gods (esp. Varuṇa), was freed, and was adopted by Viśvāmitra as his son Devarāta.

10.095.legend: King Purūravā and the apsaras Urvaśī: their union, her departure on the breaking of a condition, and his lament — the dialogue of RV 10.95.

10.010.legend: The twins Yama and Yamī, children of Vivasvat: Yamī urges union, Yama refuses on the ground of kinship — the dialogue of RV 10.10.

10.108.legend: Saramā, Indra's messenger, crosses the Rasā to the Paṇis, who hold the stolen/hidden cows, and demands their return — the dialogue of RV 10.108.

1.179.legend: The ṛṣi Agastya and his wife Lopāmudrā: her appeal and his response — the dialogue of RV 1.179.
